commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Even as the gazelle and the hart is eaten, so you shall eat them: the unclean and the clean shall eat of them alike. This must be understood of those who had contracted only a smaller stain, which did not communicate the uncleanness to others, but debarred people from approaching to sacred things. (Calmet) Those who had touched the dead, were not allowed to eat with people, who were not under any such legal uncleanness. (Menochius).
